ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Dum Ke Aloo Bharwan (stuffed slow-cooked potato). FOODON_03311737 (potato dish) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Potato products and dishes) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for stuffed dum-style potato preparations.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Indian stuffed potato preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Dum Ke Aloo Bharwan is approximately 250 g, comprising two to three whole stuffed potatoes in a spiced gravy.

nutrient_source.title: Aloo dum / Stuffed potato curry — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition), adapted with USDA FoodData Central reference values for nuts and potato components
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN); USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN provides values for dum aloo and stuffed potato curry preparations. The stuffing component (nuts, pomegranate seeds, spiced paste) increases fat, protein, and energy relative to plain dum aloo. Values are adapted from ICMR-NIN dum aloo baseline (approx. 95 kcal/100 g) upward to reflect nut-enriched stuffing, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for cashew, almond, and potato. Final per-100 g values represent a weighted composite estimate for a nut-stuffed dum potato dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous estimate based on boiled potato with mixed ingredients (GI ~52 for potato-based mixed dish with fat and protein moderating effect)
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Dum Ke Aloo Bharwan. GI estimated at 52 based on boiled/cooked potato dishes with fat and protein-rich stuffing components. The University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) report GI values for potato-based mixed dishes in the range 45–65. The presence of nuts (fat and protein) and spiced gravy (fat, fibre) lowers the effective GI relative to plain boiled potato (GI ~78). An estimate of 52 (low category, ≤55) is defensible for this preparation. GL per serving = (52 × 14.5 × 2.5) / 100 = 18.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g; ~775 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one stuffed potato and request reduced salt preparation, as a full restaurant serving can contribute approximately 775 mg sodium, representing about one-third of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) and moderate carbohydrate load (~36 g per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The nut-enriched stuffing and spiced gravy moderate the glycemic response relative to plain potato; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit serving size to one stuffed potato (~125 g) and pair with a high-fibre accompaniment to keep postprandial glucose rise within target range.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content with nuts providing unsaturated fats; ghee or oil adds saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The unsaturated fat from cashews and almonds in the stuffing is cardioprotective, but the use of ghee or oil in the gravy contributes saturated fat (~4.5 g per serving); individuals with cardiovascular disease should request preparation with minimal ghee and consume as an occasional dish rather than a daily staple.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dum Ke Aloo Bharwan is a North Indian / Mughal-inspired vegetarian dish consisting of whole potatoes that are hollowed out and filled with a rich stuffing of mixed nuts (cashews, almonds), pomegranate seeds, paneer or khoya, and aromatic spices, then slow-cooked (dum) in a sealed pot over low heat in a spiced tomato-onion-yoghurt gravy. The dum cooking technique — sealing the pot with dough or a tight lid to trap steam — produces an intensely flavoured, tender potato with a moist, fragrant interior. The texture is soft and yielding, with the stuffing providing a contrasting richness from the nuts and a subtle tartness from the pomegranate seeds. The flavour profile is complex: earthy from the potato, nutty and creamy from the stuffing, warmly spiced with cumin, coriander, garam masala, and chilli, and slightly tangy from yoghurt in the gravy. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate energy (~128 kcal/100 g), meaningful dietary fibre (~2.1 g/100 g), potassium (~420 mg/100 g), and iron (~1.2 mg/100 g) from the potato and nut components. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 52 (low category), moderated by the fat and protein from nuts and dairy. Sodium is moderate at ~310 mg/100 g, rising to approximately 775 mg per restaurant serving, which is clinically relevant for hypertension management. The dish is vegetarian, halal, and gluten-free, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary requirements. It is typically served as a main course alongside naan, roti, or rice in Indian restaurant settings.

confidence_reason: No exact match for Dum Ke Aloo Bharwan exists in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN dum aloo baseline with upward adjustment for nut-enriched stuffing, cross-referenced with USDA component data. GI is an analogous estimate based on potato-based mixed dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2021). Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates adapted from ICMR-NIN dum aloo and USDA FoodData Central nut/potato component data; no single approved source entry exactly matches this restaurant preparation. 2. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this is a vegetarian dish with no meat, fish, or egg components; trace dairy (yoghurt, ghee) contributes negligible B12 below reporting threshold — confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN vegetarian dish conventions. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this is a plant-based dish with no significant vitamin D source; confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN and USDA data for potato and nut components. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as no hydrogenated fat is used in traditional preparation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for the component ingredients. 5. GI estimated at 52 (low, ≤55) based on analogous potato-based mixed dish with fat and protein moderating effect per Atkinson et al. (2021) and University of Sydney GI Database. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and all micronutrient sentinel values (-1) are not individually reported in ICMR-NIN for this dish class; values for MUFA and PUFA are estimated from USDA component data for cashew and almond. 7. Serving size of 250 g is an estimate for a typical restaurant portion of two to three stuffed potatoes; actual restaurant portion may vary. 8. The input description ('breathe in pot') is interpreted as a colloquial reference to the dum (slow steam-cooking) technique.
